items.title: Miner ERG Weighs Breakup to Split Assets Between Its Owners
items.description: The owners of Eurasian Resources Group are considering splitting the mining group into two businesses, in an overhaul that would separate its Kazakh and international operations.

items.title: SK Chairman Promises ‘Much, Much Bigger’ US Investment Plan
items.description: SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won, fresh off a landmark US stock offering for the conglomerate’s memory-chip business, said he has an ambitious plan to invest more money in America.

items.title: SK Hynix Open to Issuing More US Shares, SK Group Chairman Says
items.description: SK Hynix Inc. is open to issuing more US shares should returns prove strong and the company’s stock price remains stable, SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won said.

items.title: Europeans Are Changing Saving Habits to Invest More, ING Says
items.description: Europeans are showing a durable shift in savings habits toward products such as investment funds that might one day help support greater spending, ING economists said.

items.title: SK Hynix ADRs Surge 14% After Record $26.5 Billion US Offering
items.description: SK Hynix Inc.’s American depositary receipts surged 14% above their offering price, after the South Korean memory chipmaker raised $26.5 billion in the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company.

items.title: Prediction Market Bets Surge on World Cup Mania

items.title: SK Hynix Set to Make Record-Setting US Debut
items.description: SK Hynix Inc. raised $26.5 billion in its American depositary share offering, the largest ever US first-time share sale by a foreign company. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Retail Traders Chase Shiny Objects But Refuse to Bet on S&P 500
items.description: Retail traders, some of the staunchest supporters of the stock market this decade, are showing signs of waning conviction.

items.title: AlphaQuest Founder Lost Control at Quant Fund, Investor Alleges
items.description: AlphaQuest founder Nigol Koulajian admitted losing control of trading at his $2 billion quantitative hedge fund before announcing plans to shut it down, an institutional investor claimed in a lawsuit.
